39.reason: [This condition causes early return for isolated vertices or when the vertex has no neighbors, but the problem states the graph is connected before and after deletion (except the deleted vertex). However, more critically, it skips printing vertex 0 if it has no neighbors鈥攚hich shouldn't happen鈥攂ut worse: even if a vertex is valid and should be printed (like start vertex 0), this returns early without printing. The print statement must happen regardless of whether the vertex has neighbors.]

121.reason: [This line attempts to free g[vc].link, but if g[vc].link is NULL (e.g., if vc had no edges), calling free(NULL) is safe, so this is not an error. However, the real issue is that after deletion, the DFS and BFS functions may not handle the deleted vertex correctly due to logic errors in traversal functions.]
